emails.subject: URGENT: Security Alert - Potential Web Server Intrusion Detected
emails.body:
Hi Alice,

Our intrusion detection system has flagged suspicious activity on the production web server. We need your immediate assistance with incident response.

**INCIDENT DETAILS:**
- Detection Time: Today at 14:32 UTC
- Affected System: Production web server
- Location: /var/www directory
- Severity: HIGH

**WHAT WE DETECTED:**
Our monitoring picked up unusual POST requests and what appears to be command injection attempts. The security logs show new files being created in unexpected locations.

**REQUESTED ACTIONS:**
1. Scan the /var/www directory for any files modified in the last 24 hours
2. Review the security access logs for correlation with the alerts
3. Identify and quarantine any suspicious files (look for webshells, backdoors, or unauthorized modifications)
4. Create a quarantine folder at /home/alice/quarantine for isolation
5. Document everything in an incident report at /var/log/security/incident_report.txt
6. Maintain a log of all actions taken

Please reply to this email with your findings once the investigation is complete. We need to determine if this was a successful breach or just an attempted attack.

The on-call team is monitoring #security-ops on Slack - please post updates there as well.

Stay vigilant,
IT Security Team

emails.subject: Re: Website deployment yesterday
emails.body:
Hey Alice,

Just wanted to follow up on yesterday's deployment. Everything looked good on my end. The new feature is live and working as expected.

Let me know if you see any issues!

Bob
